info.title: Meristem Application Management
info.description: Meristem Management for managing users, agents, stocks

tags.name: Enquiry — Holder
tags.description: Full holder profile with historical sub-records (certificates, dividends, transfers, changes, mergers, admon, statements)

tags.name: Role & Permission Management
tags.description: Secured APIs for managing user access levels, system definitions, and associated authority matrices. Restricted to SUPER_ADMIN.

tags.name: IPO Audit & Compliance Reporting
tags.description: APIs driving back-office report sheets including subscription distribution matrices, geopolitical regional breakdowns, allocation range slices, and direct ledger data-stream downloads

tags.name: Approvals
tags.description: Maker-checker approval queue — submit, action, delegate, recall, resubmit, and batch-process approval items across all modules (Dividends, Certificates, Account Maintenance, Offers, Setup). Tiered workflow from OPS_MANAGER (Tier 2) through ICU_OFFICER → MD → CEO (Tier 4).

tags.name: CSCS Transaction & Settlement Management
tags.description: APIs driving the central securities clearing system pipelines, including file ingestion logs, continuous settlement processing queues, and ghost-seller exception risk management workflows

put.description: Idempotently replaces all previously assigned security roles for the target user profile with the incoming collection request data.

post.description: Appends new system role definitions to an existing user account index without clearing or altering their existing active roles.

put.description: Overwrites all explicitly attached direct permission points assigned to a single profile context block.

post.description: Appends specific direct permission flags onto the target user schema profile to craft exceptional access parameters above assigned role templates.

put.description: Performs partial modification and field merges on fundamental demographic details linked with a targeted user registration model.
Replaces the target user's full role set. Refuses to strip SUPER_ADMIN off the last remaining super admin.
Idempotently overwrites and replaces all existing permission links associated with the specified role identifier with the incoming raw dataset collection.

post.description: Appends specific permission entries to an existing role architecture without purging or affecting its current assignment state.

delete.description: Selectively dissects and unlinks explicitly identified permission authorities away from the target system role index matrix.

put.description: Update an existing register with the provided details

delete.description: Delete a register by Register ID

put.description: Update an existing principal with the provided details
Updates an IPO offer — only allowed while status is DRAFT
Single approval step. Requires the 'Authorise Consolidation' permission. The original submitter cannot approve their own request (maker-checker).

get.description: Retrieve a specific agent by database ID

put.description: Update an existing agent with the provided details

get.description: Paginated, filterable transaction register.

post.description: Streams a CSV file containing the selected transaction records.

post.summary: Make a user a super admin
post.description: Grants the SUPER_ADMIN role to the target user. Idempotent.

delete.summary: Remove super admin from a user
delete.description: Revokes the SUPER_ADMIN role. Refuses to remove the last remaining super admin.

get.description: Fetches a comprehensive collection of all defined roles currently configured in the system.

post.description: Provisions a brand new role signature inside the system schema layer based on validation requirements.
Executes a report. Auto-escalates to async if > 10,000 rows.
Queues report generation for large datasets

post.description: Create a new system permission. Requires ADMIN role and 'role:management' authority
Public endpoint that sets a new password using the token received via the forgot-password reset email link
Public endpoint that triggers a password reset email. Always returns 200 regardless of whether the email is registered, to avoid leaking account existence
Allows an authenticated user to change their own password. The user's email is resolved from the JWT principal — it does not need to be included in the request body

get.summary: Get all caution reasons with pagination

post.summary: Verify transactional OTP and issue session token
post.description: Public endpoint that validates an email-linked challenge token. Successful resolution yields an authenticated session envelope carrying user metadata alongside a bearer JWT.
Public endpoint that creates a time-sensitive authorization token based on a contextual system reason and triggers its delivery to the user's registered email channel.

get.description: Lists agents assigned to an offer, optionally filtered by agent type. offerType must be 'ipo'.

post.description: Associates an agent (bank/stockbroker/collecting agent) with an offer for commission tracking. offerType must be 'ipo'.

Marks a single notification as read. Pass `performedBy` (email) as a query param — mirrors the submittedBy pattern.
Marks all unread notifications as read. Pass `performedBy` (email) in the request body to scope to the calling user. Optionally set `type` to mark only a specific category.
Generates the lodgement file for the requested format and marks the batch as LODGED. Stub — no live CSCS HTTP integration yet.
Accepts a single subscription CSV file plus JSON metadata (offerId, offerName, register, batchDate). Each row is auto-classified into APPROVED / DISAPPROVED / INVALID based on data quality rules.

get.summary: Get signature on file for a holder

post.summary: Upload or update a holder's signature
post.description: Creates a new signature record if none exists, or updates the existing one.
Processes a batch collection of unstructured physical addresses through an internal regional lookup layer to determine their corresponding states.

get.summary: List KYC documents for a holder
get.description: Returns all KYC documents on file for the given CHN, optionally filtered by register.

post.summary: Upload KYC documents for a holder
post.description: Accepts one or more KYC documents in a single request. Each document requires a documentType and documentName.
Upload a single mandate form for an agent. Fields: signatoryName, position, register, email, phone, uploadedBy. Files: signature (image, max 10 MB), document (PDF, max 10 MB).

parameters.name: register
parameters.description: Register symbol the mandate applies to

post.description: Upload multiple mandates at once. Provide the filled CSV template as 'csv' and a ZIP file containing all referenced signature and document files as 'zip'. File names in the CSV must match the ZIP entry names exactly. Returns a per-row result showing SUCCESS or FAILED with a reason.

csv.description: Filled CSV file (use /mandates/bulk/template to download the template)

zip.description: ZIP file containing all signature and document files referenced in the CSV

post.summary: Approve mark-off — advances to next tier

get.summary: Load unpaid warrants for en-bloc mark-off

post.summary: Submit bulk mark-off records for approval (§11.13)
post.description: Pass the matched rows returned by the preview endpoint along with submittedBy (email) and a reason. Only rows that resolve to an UNPAID warrant are submitted; others are silently skipped. Each submitted row creates a WarrantMarkOff record at PENDING_FIRST_APPROVAL and registers it in the central approval queue.

Upload a CSV or Excel file with three columns — register_id, dividend_number, shareholder_account_number — and get back a row-by-row preview showing which rows are MATCHED (warrant found and UNPAID) and which are NO_MATCH. Unmatched rows will be skipped when submitted.

post.summary: Re-push failed records from payment run
Push approved mandate payment to NIBSS
[DEPRECATED] Seed mandate payment queue from warrants
post.description: **Deprecated** — the queue is now seeded automatically on BANK KYC approval. This endpoint will be removed in a future release.

post.summary: Batch push mandate payments to NIBSS

get.description: Paginated records with optional filters.

post.description: Creates a new dematerialisation record. Initial status: DRAFT
Allows operators to insert a missing BUY or SELL transaction for an existing holder. Validates that the holder exists and that a SELL does not result in a negative balance.
Receives a multipart ZIP archive containing nested depository data fields, unzips the file tree hierarchy dynamically, and reconciles the embedded shareholder records.

get.summary: List consolidation requests with optional filters
get.description: Use `source_accounts_unavailable=true` to return only consolidations where no source account IDs are recorded (e.g. legacy or corrupted records). Omit the parameter or pass `false` to return all records matching the other filters.

post.summary: Submit a new account consolidation request
Bulk upload consolidation requests from CSV. registerId is optional — if omitted, the register is derived from each row's destination account.

get.description: Paginated, multi-filter list of certificates. Filter by registerId, shareholderId, accountNumber, status, certNumber, or date range.

post.description: Issue a new share certificate for a shareholder. The certNumber must be unique within the register.

get.description: Paginated list of transfer requests, filterable by status, register, date range, and free-text search.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Partial match on cert number, account, or holder name

post.description: Transfer ownership of a certificate (or portion) from one shareholder to another. An Instrument of Transfer reference and stamp duty are required. Creates a PENDING approval item for dual-control authorisation.
Reject a PENDING transfer request. A comment is required.
Authorise a PENDING transfer. On approval: source cert → TRANSFERRED, new ACTIVE cert issued to transferee. If partial, a residual cert is created for the balance.
Process multiple PENDING transfer requests in a single call.

get.description: Paginated list of split requests, filterable by status, register, and date range.

post.description: Split one ACTIVE certificate into 2–4 new certificates. Creates a PENDING approval item for dual-control authorisation.
Reject a PENDING split request. A comment is required.
Authorise a PENDING split. On approval: source cert → SPLIT, new ACTIVE certs created per split part.
Process multiple PENDING split requests in a single call. approveIds are approved; rejectIds are rejected with the shared rejectComment.

get.description: Paginated list of consolidation requests, filterable by status, register, and date range.

post.description: Merge 2–50 ACTIVE certificates for the same shareholder/register into one new certificate. Creates a PENDING approval item.
Reject a PENDING consolidation request. A comment is required.
Authorise a PENDING consolidation. On approval: all source certs → SPLIT (consumed), one new ACTIVE cert created.
Process multiple PENDING consolidation requests in one call.
Validate user credentials and send a verification email upon successful login
Create a new user account with the provided registration details. Restricted to SUPER_ADMIN.
Original initiator corrects and resubmits a REJECTED item. `summaryOfChanges` and `resubmittedBy` (email) are required. Original is marked RESUBMITTED; a fresh chain is created.
Rejects the current pending step. `comment` and `authorisedBy` are required. Rejection terminates all subsequent steps and notifies the initiator.
Original initiator withdraws their submission before any step is actioned. Pass `recalledBy` (email) in the request body. Cannot recall if any approver has already approved a step.
Attaches a document to a PENDING approval item. Accepted: PDF, DOC, DOCX, JPG, PNG. Max 10 MB.

parameters.name: uploadedBy
parameters.description: Email of the uploader — mirrors submittedBy pattern

file.description: Document file (PDF, DOC, DOCX, JPG, PNG — max 10 MB)

409.description: Cannot upload to a non-PENDING item

post.description: Transfers approval responsibility to another eligible user. Pass `delegatedBy` (email) in the request body. The delegate must hold the same role or higher than the current step.

Actions the current pending step as APPROVED. Pass `authorisedBy` (email) in the request body. The initiator cannot approve their own submission (MAKER_CHECKER_VIOLATION). On the final step the item is fully approved and the payload is applied.
Reject multiple PENDING items in one call. `comment` and `rejectedBy` (email) are required.
Approve multiple PENDING items in one call. Pass `approvedBy` (email) in the request body. Items where the caller is the initiator are automatically skipped.

patch.summary: Toggle user account operational status string
patch.description: Transitions a specific user profile context through operational lifecycle states like Active, Inactive, or Pending updates.
Inverts the fundamental framework flag tracking whether a user account context is allowed to pass basic authentication check sequences.
Alters multi-factor verification checks required from a designated profile entry node upon matching authentication criteria.
Partial update — only supplied fields are changed. batchRef is immutable.

get.description: Full role detail: permissions as a flat key list plus a nested module → submodule → {read, write, approve} tree.

delete.description: Deletes a designated system role entirely from active persistence. Should fail gracefully if currently anchored to active user configurations.

patch.description: Updates name/description and REPLACES the permission set (permissionNames + moduleFullAccess). Built-in roles return 422.
Executes a differential metadata merge operation to update foundational fields on the targeted role resource structure without disrupting state maps.
Update the status of a register (Active/Inactive/Transaction Disabled)
Update the status of a principal (Active/Inactive)

OPS approves or rejects INVALID/DISAPPROVED subscribers for refund in bulk. If subscriberIds is provided, only those subscribers are acted on. Otherwise all PENDING_OPS_REVIEW INVALID + DISAPPROVED in the batch are targeted. Approve → PENDING_ICU_REVIEW | Reject → OPS_REJECTED.

ICU approves or rejects PENDING_ICU_REVIEW subscribers in bulk. If subscriberIds is provided, only those subscribers are acted on. Otherwise all PENDING_ICU_REVIEW in the batch are targeted. Approve → ELIGIBLE_FOR_REFUND | Reject → ICU_REJECTED.

patch.summary: Reject batch context from Operations layer
patch.description: Halts advancement processing over an incoming upload file and returns it to an error loop state; explicit reasoning comments are mandated.
Signs off a given batch configuration, transitioning it forward into the secondary internal validation layer (ICU).
Logs final assessment determinations over an operational bundle to push it directly into the selectable lodgment buckets.
Closes out a specific batch context manually inside the ledger state track when files have been physically submitted or verified outside system mechanisms.
Executes an immediate transaction update mapping explicitly designated state markers over a batch array of target shareholder primary keys.
Rejects from any stage except LODGED/REJECTED.
ICU_APPROVED → LODGED. Caller specifies RIN status and lodgment method (DOWNLOAD or PUSH).
AUTHORISED → ICU_APPROVED
DRAFT → CALLOVER
CALLOVER → AUTHORISED
"Reject Selected" on Authorisation and ICU Approval stages.
"Approve Selected" on ICU Approval stage.
"Approve Selected" on Authorisation stage.
Marks the consolidation as REVERSED, restores original source-account unit balances from the JSON snapshot, and clears each source holder's CONSOLIDATED status. A reversal comment is mandatory.
Mark an ACTIVE certificate as DISABLED. This action is irreversible. A reason of at least 10 characters is required.
Update the status of an agent (Active/Inactive)

Returns the user's notifications (excludes soft-deleted). Pass `performedBy` (email) to scope results. Filter by type, read status, or module. Ordered by createdAt DESC.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: APPROVAL_PENDING | APPROVAL_APPROVED | APPROVAL_REJECTED | CSCS_BATCH | EMAIL_DISPATCH | TRANSFER_COMPLETE | SYSTEM_ALERT

parameters.name: read
parameters.description: true = read only | false = unread only | omit = all

parameters.name: module
parameters.description: SETUP | DIVIDENDS | CERTIFICATES | ACCOUNT_MAINTENANCE | OFFERS | SYSTEM

parameters.name: performedBy
parameters.description: Email of the authenticated user — mirrors performedBy pattern

get.description: Returns unread count, pending approvals count, and system alerts count. Pass `performedBy` (email) to scope to a specific user.

Opens a Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream scoped to the authenticated user. The server pushes three event types:
- **notification** — a new in-app notification
- **ping** — keepalive heartbeat every 30 seconds
- **badge-update** — unread count changed (e.g. after mark-all-read in another tab)

The server sends `retry: 5000`. Clients must reconnect automatically on disconnect and pass `Last-Event-ID` (ISO-8601 timestamp of the last received event) to replay any missed notifications. Connections are terminated after 30 minutes; maximum 5 concurrent connections per user (HTTP 429 if exceeded).

parameters.name: performedBy
parameters.description: Email of the authenticated user — mirrors performedBy pattern

parameters.name: Last-Event-ID
parameters.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last received notification — used to replay missed events after reconnect

get.description: Generates a structured breakdown tracking absolute volumes and percentage ratios of public offer subscription volumes grouped cleanly across states.
Compiles and streams a tabular comma-separated layout tracking geographical investor concentrations.
Groups subscription blocks into tier ranges (e.g., 1-1000 units) to analyze demographics, density maps, and capital concentrations.

get.summary: Export allotment range analysis to CSV
get.description: Downloads the density block distributions showing investor stratification and equity funding spreads.
Provides a paginated exhaustive audit ledger mapping name indices, clearing houses data (CHN), CSCS codes, and final share allotment numbers.

get.summary: Export master subscription ledger to CSV
get.description: Extracts the complete subscription record sequence up to extreme bounds to assemble a detailed master corporate spreadsheet download file.
Returns historical lists of processed data-chunk batches along with tracking markers indicating validation velocities and financial aggregate maps.

get.summary: Export comprehensive batch metrics to CSV
get.description: Compiles high-level state history tracking matrices for uploading operations logs, mapping transaction metrics directly to string response buffers.
Retrieves a filterable paged matrix view of basic subscriber records compiled across historical register allocations.

get.summary: Export application offer rows to CSV
get.description: Gathers all underlying raw application offer data for a specific register and flushes it straight into a downloadable binary spreadsheet attachment stream.
Aggregates subscription trends across participating broker houses, tracking total volumes alongside item status metrics (Approved/Disapproved/Invalid).

get.summary: Export broker application summaries to CSV
get.description: Assembles stockbroker summary matrices and dynamically writes a comma-delimited string layout with integrated total summation footer labels.
Consolidated, filterable batch listing — by status, register, and date range.
Retrieves aggregated counts, ledger states, values, and verification messages for a target batch record reference.

get.summary: Get paginated subscribers by classification type
get.description: Paginates through individual subscription rows nested inside a batch based on sub-categories (APPROVED, DISAPPROVED, INVALID).

get.summary: Get INVALID and DISAPPROVED subscribers for refund
get.description: Returns paginated INVALID and DISAPPROVED subscribers for a batch. Optionally filter by refundStatus and/or type (DISAPPROVED | INVALID). Omit both to see all.

parameters.name: refundStatus
parameters.description: Filter by refund status — omit to return all

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by subscriber type — DISAPPROVED | INVALID

get.description: Generates foundational header calculations accompanied by a slice table configuration of top-row listings for grid sanity inspections prior to generation output.
Generates and streams a specialized fixed-width or formatted plain text file built specifically for ingestion systems (e.g., CSCS layouts) matching the requested format strategy.
Compiles and streams a downloadable CSV of subscribers of the given classification type (APPROVED, DISAPPROVED, INVALID).
Returns the 19-column CSV header used by the batch upload endpoint.
Retrieves a filterable, chunked database view of corporate shareholder records matching complex lookup predicates like name, CHN, or register context boundaries.

parameters.name: warrantNo
parameters.description: Warrant number (required if accountNo not provided)

parameters.name: accountNo
parameters.description: Account number (required if warrantNo not provided)

get.description: Returns full warrant detail including bank and payment information.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort field and direction, e.g. createdAt,desc

parameters.description: Scope to a single register by symbol

Downloads a CSV template with columns: signatory_name, position, register, email, phone, signature_filename, document_filename. Fill in one row per mandate. Place the referenced signature and document files into a ZIP file (filenames must match exactly), then upload both the CSV and ZIP via the bulk upload endpoint.

Returns a paginated list of approval items. Use `scope=MY_DESK` to see items pending your role, or `scope=GLOBAL` for the system-wide queue (requires OPS_MANAGER or above). INITIATOR role may only use MY_DESK and sees only their own submissions.

delete.description: Selectively unlinks a designated role identifier away from a distinct user profile context state.
